content.content:
1→---
     2→name: qa
     3→description: "Code review + QA vs Acceptance Criteria, including A+ status suggestions and review comment draft."
     4→license: MIT
     5→metadata:
     6→  author: sequant
     7→  version: "1.0"
     8→allowed-tools:
     9→  - Bash(npm test:*)
    10→  - Bash(npm run build:*)
    11→  - Bash(git diff:*)
    12→  - Bash(git status:*)
    13→  - Bash(gh issue view:*)
    14→  - Bash(gh issue comment:*)
    15→  - Bash(gh issue edit:*)
    16→  - Bash(gh pr view:*)
    17→  - Bash(gh pr diff:*)
    18→  - Bash(gh pr comment:*)
    19→  - Bash(gh pr checks:*)
    20→  - Bash(semgrep:*)
    21→  - Bash(npx semgrep:*)
    22→  - Bash(npx tsx scripts/semgrep-scan.ts:*)
    23→  - Task(general-purpose)
    24→  - AgentOutputTool
    25→---
    26→
    27→# QA & Code Review
    28→
    29→You are the Phase 3 "QA & Code Review Agent" for the current repository.
    30→
    31→## Purpose
    32→
    33→When invoked as `/qa`, your job is to:
    34→
    35→1. Review the current state of the implementation for a single issue.
    36→2. Perform a focused code review for correctness, readability, and alignment with repo standards.
    37→3. Validate behavior against the Acceptance Criteria (AC).
    38→4. Assess whether the change is "A+ status" or needs more work.
    39→5. Draft a GitHub review/QA comment summarizing findings and recommendations.
    40→
    41→## Orchestration Context
    42→
    43→When running as part of an orchestrated workflow (e.g., `sequant run` or `/fullsolve`), this skill receives environment variables that indicate the orchestration context:
    44→
    45→| Environment Variable | Description | Example Value |
    46→|---------------------|-------------|---------------|
    47→| `SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR` | The orchestrator invoking this skill | `sequant-run` |
    48→| `SEQUANT_PHASE` | Current phase in the workflow | `qa` |
    49→| `SEQUANT_ISSUE` | Issue number being processed | `123` |
    50→| `SEQUANT_WORKTREE` | Path to the feature worktree | `/path/to/worktrees/feature/...` |
    51→
    52→**Behavior when orchestrated (SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR is set):**
    53→
    54→1. **Skip pre-flight sync check** - Orchestrator has already synced
    55→2. **Use provided worktree** - Work in `SEQUANT_WORKTREE` path directly
    56→3. **Skip issue fetch** - Use `SEQUANT_ISSUE`, orchestrator has context
    57→4. **Reduce GitHub comment frequency** - Defer updates to orchestrator
    58→5. **Trust git state** - Orchestrator verified branch status
    59→
    60→**Behavior when standalone (SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR is NOT set):**
    61→
    62→- Perform pre-flight sync check
    63→- Locate worktree or work from main
    64→- Fetch fresh issue context from GitHub
    65→- Post QA comment directly to GitHub
    66→
    67→## Phase Detection (Smart Resumption)
    68→
    69→**Before executing**, check if the exec phase has been completed (prerequisite for QA):
    70→
    71→```bash
    72→# Check for existing phase markers
    73→comments_json=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --json comments --jq '[.comments[].body]')
    74→exec_completed=$(echo "$comments_json" | \
    75→  grep -o '{[^}]*}' | grep '"phase"' | \
    76→  jq -r 'select(.phase == "exec" and .status == "completed")' 2>/dev/null || true)
    77→
    78→if [[ -z "$exec_completed" ]]; then
    79→  # Check if any exec marker exists at all
    80→  exec_any=$(echo "$comments_json" | \
    81→    grep -o '{[^}]*}' | grep '"phase"' | \
    82→    jq -r 'select(.phase == "exec")' 2>/dev/null || true)
    83→
    84→  if [[ -n "$exec_any" ]]; then
    85→    echo "⚠️ Exec phase not completed (status: $(echo "$exec_any" | jq -r '.status')). Run /exec first."
    86→  else
    87→    echo "ℹ️ No phase markers found — proceeding with QA (may be a fresh issue or legacy workflow)."
    88→  fi
    89→fi
    90→```
    91→
    92→**Behavior:**
    93→- If `exec:completed` marker found → Normal QA execution
    94→- If `exec:failed` or `exec:in_progress` → Warn "Exec not complete, run /exec first" (but don't block — QA may still be useful for partial review)
    95→- If no markers found → Normal execution (backward compatible)
    96→- If detection fails (API error) → Fall through to normal execution
    97→
    98→**Phase Marker Emission:**
    99→
   100→When posting the QA review comment to GitHub, append a phase marker at the end:
   101→
   102→```markdown
   103→<!-- SEQUANT_PHASE: {"phase":"qa","status":"completed","timestamp":"<ISO-8601>"} -->
   104→```
   105→
   106→If QA determines AC_NOT_MET, emit:
   107→```markdown
   108→<!-- SEQUANT_PHASE: {"phase":"qa","status":"failed","timestamp":"<ISO-8601>","error":"AC_NOT_MET"} -->
   109→```
   110→
   111→Include this marker in every `gh issue comment` that represents QA completion.
   112→
   113→## Behavior
   114→
   115→Invocation:
   116→
   117→- `/qa 123`: Treat `123` as the GitHub issue/PR identifier in context.
   118→- `/qa <freeform description>`: Treat the text as context about the change to review.
   119→- `/qa 123 --parallel`: Force parallel agent execution (faster, higher token usage).
   120→- `/qa 123 --sequential`: Force sequential agent execution (slower, lower token usage).
   121→
   122→### Agent Execution Mode
   123→
   124→Before spawning quality check agents, determine the execution mode:
   125→
   126→1. **Check for CLI flag override:**
   127→   - `--parallel` → Use parallel execution
   128→   - `--sequential` → Use sequential execution
   129→
   130→2. **If no flag, read project settings:**
   131→   Use the Read tool to check project settings:
   132→   ```
   133→   Read(file_path=".sequant/settings.json")
   134→   # Parse JSON and extract agents.parallel (default: false)
   135→   ```
   136→
   137→3. **Default:** Sequential (cost-optimized)
   138→
   139→| Mode | Token Usage | Speed | Best For |
   140→|------|-------------|-------|----------|
   141→| Sequential | 1x (baseline) | Slower | Limited API plans, single issues |
   142→| Parallel | ~2-3x | ~50% faster | Unlimited plans, batch operations |
   143→
   144→### Quality Check Caching
   145→
   146→The QA quality checks support caching to skip unchanged checks on re-run, significantly improving iteration speed.
   147→
   148→#### Cache Configuration
   149→
   150→**CLI flags:**
   151→- `/qa 123 --no-cache`: Force fresh run, ignore all cached results
   152→- `/qa 123 --use-cache`: Enable caching (default)
   153→
   154→**When caching is used:**
   155→- Type safety check → Cached (keyed by diff hash)
   156→- Deleted tests check → Cached (keyed by diff hash)
   157→- Security scan → Cached (keyed by diff hash + config)
   158→- Semgrep analysis → Cached (keyed by diff hash)
   159→- Build verification → Cached (keyed by diff hash)
   160→- Scope/size metrics → Always fresh (cheap operations)
   161→
   162→#### Cache Invalidation Rules
   163→
   164→| Change Type | Invalidation Scope |
   165→|-------------|-------------------|
   166→| Source file changes | Re-run type safety, security, semgrep |
   167→| Test file changes | Re-run deleted-tests check |
   168→| Config changes (tsconfig, package.json) | Re-run affected checks |
   169→| `package-lock.json` changes | Re-run ALL checks |
   170→| TTL expiry (1 hour default) | Re-run expired checks |
   171→
   172→#### Cache Status Reporting (AC-4)
   173→
   174→The quality-checks.sh script outputs a cache status table:
   175→
   176→```markdown
   177→### Cache Status Report
   178→
   179→| Check | Cache Status |
   180→|-------|--------------|
   181→| type-safety | ✅ HIT |
   182→| deleted-tests | ✅ HIT |
   183→| scope | ⏭️ SKIP |
   184→| size | ⏭️ SKIP |
   185→| security | ❌ MISS |
   186→| semgrep | ❌ MISS |
   187→| build | ✅ HIT |
   188→
   189→**Summary:** 3 hits, 2 misses, 2 skipped
   190→**Performance:** Cached checks saved execution time
   191→```
   192→
   193→#### Cache Location
   194→
   195→Cache is stored at `.sequant/.cache/qa/cache.json` with the following structure:
   196→- `diffHash`: SHA256 hash of `git diff main...HEAD`
   197→- `configHash`: SHA256 hash of relevant config files
   198→- `result`: Check result (passed, message, details)
   199→- `ttl`: Time-to-live in milliseconds (default: 1 hour)
   200→
   201→#### Graceful Degradation (AC-6)
   202→
   203→If the cache is corrupted or unreadable:
   204→1. Log warning at debug level (AC-7)
   205→2. Fall back to fresh run
   206→3. Continue without caching errors affecting QA
   207→
   208→### Pre-flight Sync Check
   209→
   210→**Skip this section if `SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR` is set** - the orchestrator has already verified sync status.
   211→
   212→Before starting QA (standalone mode), verify the local branch is in sync with remote:
   213→
   214→```bash
   215→git fetch origin 2>/dev/null || echo "Network unavailable - proceeding with local state"
   216→git status -sb | head -1  # Shows ahead/behind status
   217→```
   218→
   219→**Status interpretation:**
   220→- `[ahead N]` - Local has commits not on remote (OK to proceed)
   221→- `[behind N]` - Remote has commits not pulled locally (recommend sync first)
   222→- `[ahead N, behind M]` - Branches diverged (recommend sync before QA)
   223→
   224→If diverged, recommend:
   225→```bash
   226→git pull origin main  # Or merge origin/main if pull fails
   227→```
   228→
   229→### Feature Worktree Workflow
   230→
   231→**QA Phase:** Review code in the feature worktree.
   232→
   233→**If orchestrated (SEQUANT_WORKTREE is set):**
   234→- Use the provided worktree path directly: `cd $SEQUANT_WORKTREE`
   235→- Skip step 1 below (worktree location provided by orchestrator)
   236→
   237→**If standalone:**
   238→
   239→1. **Locate the worktree:**
   240→   - The worktree should already exist from the execution phase (`/exec`)
   241→   - Find the worktree: `git worktree list` or check `../worktrees/feature/` for directories matching the issue number
   242→   - The worktree path will be: `../worktrees/feature/<issue-number>-<issue-title-slug>/`
   243→
   244→2. **Check implementation status:**
   245→   - Navigate to worktree: `cd <worktree-path>`
   246→   - Check for uncommitted changes: `git status`
   247→   - Check for committed changes: `git log --oneline main..HEAD`
   248→
   249→   **Status interpretation:**
   250→   - **No commits AND no uncommitted changes:** Implementation may not be started
   251→   - **Uncommitted changes exist:** Implementation done but not committed
   252→   - **Commits exist:** Implementation committed and ready for review
   253→
   254→3. **Review in the worktree:**
   255→   - Navigate to the worktree directory to review the implementation
   256→   - Use `git diff main...HEAD` to see all changes made in the feature branch
   257→   - Run `npm test` and `npm run build` in the worktree to verify everything works
   258→   - Review the code changes against the AC checklist
   259→
   260→4. **Pre-merge cleanup check:**
   261→   - Check for untracked files in main that match PR files: `git status --short`
   262→   - If found, compare versions and remove older local copies
   263→
   264→**Important:** Review the actual implementation in the worktree, not the main branch.
   265→
   266→### No Worktree Found
   267→
   268→If no feature worktree exists (work was done directly on main):
   269→
   270→1. **Identify relevant commits:**
   271→   ```bash
   272→   git log --oneline -10
   273→   ```
   274→
   275→2. **Find the base commit** (before the implementation started):
   276→   ```bash
   277→   # Look for the last commit before the feature work
   278→   git log --oneline --before="<date>" -1
   279→   ```
   280→
   281→3. **Review changes from base:**
   282→   ```bash
   283→   git diff <base-commit>...HEAD --name-only
   284→   git diff <base-commit>...HEAD
   285→   ```
   286→
   287→4. **Run quality checks** on the current branch instead of comparing to a worktree.
   288→
   289→### Phase 0: Implementation Status Check — REQUIRED
   290→
   291→**Before spawning quality check agents**, verify that implementation actually exists. Running full QA on an unimplemented issue wastes tokens and produces confusing output.
   292→
   293→**Detection Logic:**
   294→
   295→```bash
   296→# 1. Check for worktree (indicates work may have started)
   297→worktree_path=$(git worktree list | grep -i "<issue-number>" | awk '{print $1}' | head -1 || true)
   298→
   299→# 2. Check for commits on feature branch (vs main)
   300→commits_exist=$(git log --oneline main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | head -1)
   301→
   302→# 3. Check for uncommitted changes
   303→uncommitted_changes=$(git status --porcelain | head -1)
   304→
   305→# 4. Check for open PR linked to this issue
   306→pr_exists=$(gh pr list --search "<issue-number>" --state open --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null)
   307→```
   308→
   309→**Implementation Status Matrix:**
   310→
   311→| Worktree | Commits | Uncommitted | PR | Status | Action |
   312→|----------|---------|-------------|-----|--------|--------|
   313→| ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | No implementation | Early exit |
   314→| ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | Worktree created but no work | Early exit |
   315→| ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Work in progress (uncommitted) | Proceed with QA |
   316→| ✅ | ✅ | * | * | Implementation exists | Proceed with QA |
   317→| * | ✅ | * | * | Commits exist | Proceed with QA |
   318→| * | * | * | ✅ | PR exists | Proceed with QA |
   319→
   320→**Early Exit Condition:**
   321→- No commits on feature branch AND no uncommitted changes AND no open PR
   322→
   323→**If early exit triggered:**
   324→1. **Skip** sub-agent spawning (nothing to check)
   325→2. **Skip** code review (no code to review)
   326→3. **Skip** quality metrics collection
   327→4. Use the **Early Exit Output Template** below
   328→5. Verdict: `AC_NOT_MET`
   329→
   330→---
   331→
   332→### Early Exit Output Template
   333→
   334→When no implementation is detected, use this streamlined output:
   335→
   336→```markdown
   337→## QA Review for Issue #<N>
   338→
   339→### Implementation Status: NOT FOUND
   340→
   341→No implementation detected for this issue:
   342→- Commits on feature branch: None
   343→- Uncommitted changes: None
   344→- Open PR: None
   345→
   346→**Verdict: AC_NOT_MET**
   347→
   348→No code changes found to review. The acceptance criteria cannot be evaluated without an implementation.
   349→
   350→### Next Steps
   351→
   352→1. Run `/exec <issue-number>` to implement the feature
   353→2. Re-run `/qa <issue-number>` after implementation is complete
   354→
   355→---
   356→
   357→*QA skipped: No implementation to review*
   358→```
   359→
   360→**Important:** Do NOT spawn sub-agents when using early exit. This saves tokens and avoids confusing "no changes found" outputs from quality checkers.
   361→
   362→---
   363→
   364→### Phase 0b: Quality Plan Verification (CONDITIONAL)
   365→
   366→**When to apply:** If issue has a Feature Quality Planning section in comments (from `/spec`).
   367→
   368→**Purpose:** Verify that quality dimensions identified during planning were addressed in implementation. This catches gaps that AC verification alone misses.
   369→
   370→**Detection:**
   371→```bash
   372→# Check if issue has quality planning section in comments
   373→quality_plan_exists=$(gh issue view <issue> --comments --json comments -q '.comments[].body' | grep -q "Feature Quality Planning" && echo "yes" || echo "no")
   374→```
   375→
   376→**If Quality Plan found:**
   377→
   378→1. **Extract quality dimensions** from the spec comment:
   379→   - Completeness Check items
   380→   - Error Handling items
   381→   - Code Quality items
   382→   - Test Coverage Plan items
   383→   - Best Practices items
   384→   - Polish items (if UI feature)
   385→   - Derived ACs
   386→
   387→2. **Verify each dimension against implementation:**
   388→
   389→   | Dimension | Verification Method |
   390→   |-----------|---------------------|
   391→   | Completeness | Check all AC steps have code |
   392→   | Error Handling | Search for error handling code, try/catch blocks |
   393→   | Code Quality | Check for `any` types, magic strings |
   394→   | Test Coverage | Verify test files exist for critical paths |
   395→   | Best Practices | Check for logging, security patterns |
   396→   | Polish | Check loading/error/empty states in UI |
   397→
   398→3. **Extract and Verify Derived ACs:**
   399→
   400→   **Extraction Method:**
   401→   ```bash
   402→   # Extract derived ACs from spec comment's Derived ACs table
   403→   # Format: | Source | AC-N: Description | Priority |
   404→   # Uses flexible pattern to match any source dimension (not hardcoded)
   405→   derived_acs=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --comments --json comments -q '.comments[].body' | \
   406→     grep -E '\|[^|]+\|\s*AC-[0-9]+:' | \
   407→     grep -oE 'AC-[0-9]+:[^|]+' | \
   408→     sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | \
   409→     sort -u || true)
   410→
   411→   # Count derived ACs
   412→   derived_count=$(echo "$derived_acs" | grep -c "AC-" || echo "0")
   413→   echo "Found $derived_count derived ACs"
   414→   ```
   415→
   416→   **Handling Edge Cases:**
   417→   - **0 derived ACs:** Output "Derived ACs: None found" and skip derived AC verification
   418→   - **1+ derived ACs:** Include each in AC coverage table with source attribution
   419→   - **Malformed rows:** Rows missing the `| Source | AC-N: ... |` pattern are skipped
   420→   - **Extra whitespace:** Trimmed during extraction
   421→
   422→   **Verification:**
   423→   - Treat derived ACs identically to original ACs
   424→   - Include in AC coverage table with "Derived ([Source])" notation
   425→   - Mark as MET/PARTIALLY_MET/NOT_MET based on implementation evidence
   426→
   427→**Output Format:**
   428→
   429→```markdown
   430→### Quality Plan Verification
   431→
   432→**Quality Plan found:** Yes/No
   433→
   434→| Dimension | Items Planned | Items Addressed | Status |
   435→|-----------|---------------|-----------------|--------|
   436→| Completeness | 5 | 5 | ✅ Complete |
   437→| Error Handling | 3 | 2 | ⚠️ Partial (missing: API timeout) |
   438→| Code Quality | 4 | 4 | ✅ Complete |
   439→| Test Coverage | 3 | 3 | ✅ Complete |
   440→| Best Practices | 2 | 2 | ✅ Complete |
   441→| Polish | N/A | N/A | - (not UI feature) |
   442→
   443→**Derived ACs:** 2/2 addressed
   444→
   445→**Quality Plan Status:** Complete / Partial / Not Addressed
   446→```
   447→
   448→**Verdict Impact:**
   449→
   450→| Quality Plan Status | Verdict Impact |
   451→|---------------------|----------------|
   452→| Complete | No impact (positive signal) |
   453→| Partial | Note in findings, consider `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` |
   454→| Not Addressed | Flag in findings, may indicate gaps |
   455→| No Plan Found | Note: "Quality plan not available - standard QA only" |
   456→
   457→**Status Threshold Definitions:**
   458→
   459→| Status | Criteria |
   460→|--------|----------|
   461→| **Complete** | All applicable dimensions have ≥80% items addressed |
   462→| **Partial** | At least 50% of applicable dimensions have items addressed |
   463→| **Not Addressed** | <50% of applicable dimensions addressed, or 0 items addressed |
   464→
   465→*Example: If 4 dimensions apply (Completeness, Error Handling, Code Quality, Test Coverage):*
   466→- *Complete: 4/4 dimensions at ≥80%*
   467→- *Partial: 2-3/4 dimensions have work done*
   468→- *Not Addressed: 0-1/4 dimensions have work done*
   469→
   470→**If no Quality Plan found:**
   471→- Output: "Quality Plan Verification: N/A - No quality plan found in issue comments"
   472→- Proceed with standard QA (no verdict impact)
   473→
   474→---
   475→
   476→### Phase 1: CI Status Check — REQUIRED
   477→
   478→**Purpose:** Check GitHub CI status before finalizing verdict. CI-dependent AC items (e.g., "Tests pass in CI") should reflect actual CI status, not just local test results.
   479→
   480→**When to check:** If a PR exists for the issue/branch.
   481→
   482→**Detection:**
   483→```bash
   484→# Get PR number for current branch
   485→pr_number=$(gh pr view --json number -q '.number' 2>/dev/null)
   486→
   487→# If PR exists, check CI status
   488→if [[ -n "$pr_number" ]]; then
   489→  gh pr checks "$pr_number" --json name,state,bucket
   490→fi
   491→```
   492→
   493→**CI Status Mapping:**
   494→
   495→| State | Bucket | AC Status | Verdict Impact |
   496→|-------|--------|-----------|----------------|
   497→| `SUCCESS` | `pass` | `MET` | No impact |
   498→| `FAILURE` | `fail` | `NOT_MET` | Blocks merge |
   499→| `CANCELLED` | `fail` | `NOT_MET` | Blocks merge |
   500→| `SKIPPED` | `pass` | `N/A` | No impact |
   501→| `PENDING` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   502→| `QUEUED` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   503→| `IN_PROGRESS` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   504→| (empty response) | - | `N/A` | No CI configured |
   505→
   506→**CI-Related AC Detection:**
   507→
   508→Identify AC items that depend on CI by matching these patterns:
   509→- "Tests pass in CI"
   510→- "CI passes"
   511→- "Build succeeds in CI"
   512→- "GitHub Actions pass"
   513→- "Pipeline passes"
   514→- "Workflow passes"
   515→- "Checks pass"
   516→- "Actions succeed"
   517→- "CI/CD passes"
   518→
   519→```bash
   520→# Example: Check if any AC mentions CI
   521→ci_ac_patterns="CI|pipeline|GitHub Actions|build succeeds|tests pass in CI|workflow|checks pass|actions succeed"
   522→```
   523→
   524→**Error Handling:**
   525→
   526→If `gh pr checks` fails or returns unexpected results:
   527→- **`gh` not installed** → Skip CI section with note: "CI status unavailable (gh CLI not found)"
   528→- **`gh` not authenticated** → Skip CI section with note: "CI status unavailable (gh auth required)"
   529→- **Network/auth error** → Treat as N/A with note: "CI status unavailable (gh command failed)"
   530→- **No PR exists** → Skip CI status section entirely
   531→- **Empty response** → No CI configured (not an error)
   532→
   533→**Portability Note:**
   534→
   535→CI status detection requires GitHub. Other platforms (GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps) are not supported. To check if `gh` is available:
   536→```bash
   537→if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
   538→  echo "gh CLI not installed - skipping CI status check"
   539→fi
   540→```
   541→
   542→**Output Format:**
   543→
   544→Include CI status in the QA output:
   545→
   546→```markdown
   547→### CI Status
   548→
   549→| Check | State | Bucket | Impact |
   550→|-------|-------|--------|--------|
   551→| `build (18.x)` | SUCCESS | pass | ✅ MET |
   552→| `build (20.x)` | PENDING | pending | ⏳ PENDING |
   553→| `lint` | FAILURE | fail | ❌ NOT_MET |
   554→
   555→**CI Summary:** 1 passed, 1 pending, 1 failed
   556→**CI-related AC items:** AC-4 ("Tests pass in CI") → PENDING (CI still running)
   557→```
   558→
   559→**No CI Configured:**
   560→
   561→If `gh pr checks` returns an empty response:
   562→```markdown
   563→### CI Status
   564→
   565→No CI checks configured for this repository.
   566→
   567→**CI-related AC items:** AC-4 ("Tests pass in CI") → N/A (no CI configured)
   568→```
   569→
   570→**Verdict Integration:**
   571→
   572→CI status affects the final verdict through the standard verdict algorithm:
   573→- CI `PENDING` → AC item marked `PENDING` → Verdict: `NEEDS_VERIFICATION`
   574→- CI `failure` → AC item marked `NOT_MET` → Verdict: `AC_NOT_MET`
   575→- CI `success` → AC item marked `MET` → No additional impact
   576→- No CI → AC item marked `N/A` → No impact on verdict
   577→
   578→**Important:** Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` if any CI check is still pending. The correct verdict is `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` with a note to re-run QA after CI completes.
   579→
   580→---
   581→
   582→### Quality Checks (Multi-Agent) — REQUIRED
   583→
   584→**You MUST spawn sub-agents for quality checks.** Do NOT run these checks inline with bash commands. Sub-agents provide parallel execution, better context isolation, and consistent reporting.
   585→
   586→**Execution mode:** Respect the agent execution mode determined above (see "Agent Execution Mode" section).
   587→
   588→#### If parallel mode enabled:
   589→
   590→**Spawn ALL THREE agents in a SINGLE message (one Tool call per agent, all in same response):**
   591→
   592→1. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run type safety and deleted tests checks on the current branch vs main. Report: type issues count, deleted tests, verdict.")`
   593→
   594→2. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run scope and size checks on the current branch vs main. Report: files count, diff size, size assessment.")`
   595→
   596→3. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run security scan on changed files in current branch vs main. Report: critical/warning/info counts, verdict.")`
   597→
   598→#### If sequential mode (default):
   599→
   600→**Spawn each agent ONE AT A TIME, waiting for each to complete before the next:**
   601→
   602→1. **First:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run type safety and deleted tests checks on the current branch vs main. Report: type issues count, deleted tests, verdict.")`
   603→
   604→2. **After #1 completes:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run scope and size checks on the current branch vs main. Report: files count, diff size, size assessment.")`
   605→
   606→3. **After #2 completes:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run security scan on changed files in current branch vs main. Report: critical/warning/info counts, verdict.")`
   607→
   608→**Add RLS check if admin files modified:**
   609→```bash
   610→admin_modified=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^app/admin/" | head -1 || true)
   611→```
   612→
   613→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed verdict synthesis.
   614→
   615→### Using MCP Tools (Optional)
   616→
   617→- **Sequential Thinking:** For complex multi-step analysis
   618→- **Context7:** For broader pattern context and library documentation
   619→
   620→### 1. Context and AC Alignment
   621→
   622→- **Read all GitHub issue comments** for complete context
   623→- Reconstruct the AC checklist (AC-1, AC-2, ...)
   624→- If AC unclear, state assumptions explicitly
   625→
   626→### 2. Code Review
   627→
   628→Perform a code review focusing on:
   629→
   630→- Correctness and potential bugs
   631→- Readability and maintainability
   632→- Alignment with existing patterns (see CLAUDE.md)
   633→- TypeScript strictness and type safety
   634→- **Duplicate utility check:** Verify new utilities don't duplicate existing ones in `docs/patterns/`
   635→
   636→See [code-review-checklist.md](references/code-review-checklist.md) for integration verification steps.
   637→
   638→### 2a. Build Verification (When Build Fails)
   639→
   640→**When to apply:** `npm run build` fails on the feature branch.
   641→
   642→**Purpose:** Distinguish between pre-existing build failures (already on main) and regressions introduced by this PR.
   643→
   644→**Detection:**
   645→```bash
   646→# Run build and capture result
   647→npm run build 2>&1
   648→BUILD_EXIT_CODE=$?
   649→```
   650→
   651→**If build fails, verify against main:**
   652→
   653→The quality-checks.sh script includes `run_build_with_verification()` which:
   654→1. Runs `npm run build` on the feature branch
   655→2. If it fails, runs build on main branch (via the main repo directory)
   656→3. Compares exit codes and first error lines
   657→4. Produces a "Build Verification" table (see AC-4)
   658→
   659→**Verification Logic:**
   660→
   661→| Feature Build | Main Build | Error Match | Result |
   662→|---------------|------------|-------------|--------|
   663→| ❌ Fail | ✅ Pass | N/A | **Regression** - failure introduced by PR |
   664→| ❌ Fail | ❌ Fail | Same error | **Pre-existing** - not blocking |
   665→| ❌ Fail | ❌ Fail | Different | **Unknown** - manual review needed |
   666→| ✅ Pass | * | N/A | No verification needed |
   667→
   668→**Verdict Impact:**
   669→
   670→| Build Verification Result | Verdict Impact |
   671→|---------------------------|----------------|
   672→| Regression detected | `AC_NOT_MET` - must fix before merge |
   673→| Pre-existing failure | No impact - document and proceed |
   674→| Unknown (different errors) | `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` - manual review |
   675→| Build passes | No impact |
   676→
   677→**Output Format:**
   678→
   679→```markdown
   680→### Build Verification
   681→
   682→| Check | Status |
   683→|-------|--------|
   684→| Feature branch build | ❌ Failed |
   685→| Main branch build | ❌ Failed |
   686→| Error match | ✅ Same error |
   687→| Regression | **No** (pre-existing) |
   688→
   689→**Note:** Build failure is pre-existing on main branch. Not blocking this PR.
   690→```
   691→
   692→### 2b. Test Coverage Transparency (REQUIRED)
   693→
   694→**Purpose:** Report which changed files have corresponding tests, not just "N tests passed."
   695→
   696→**After running `npm test`, you MUST analyze test coverage for changed files:**
   697→
   698→Use the Glob tool to check for corresponding test files:
   699→```
   700→# Get changed source files (excluding tests) from git
   701→changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | grep -v -E '\.test\.|\.spec\.|__tests__' || true)
   702→
   703→# For each changed file, use the Glob tool to find matching test files
   704→# Glob(pattern="**/${base}.test.*") or Glob(pattern="**/${base}.spec.*")
   705→# If no test file found, report "NO TEST: $file"
   706→```
   707→
   708→**Required reporting format:**
   709→
   710→| Scenario | Report |
   711→|----------|--------|
   712→| Tests cover changed files | `Tests: N passed (covers changed files)` |
   713→| Tests don't cover changed files | `Tests: N passed (⚠️ 0 cover changed files)` |
   714→| No tests for specific files | `Tests: N passed (⚠️ NO TESTS: file1.ts, file2.ts)` |
   715→
   716→**Include in output template:**
   717→
   718→```markdown
   719→### Test Coverage Analysis
   720→
   721→| Changed File | Has Tests? | Test File |
   722→|--------------|------------|-----------|
   723→| `lib/foo.ts` | ✅ Yes | `__tests__/foo.test.ts` |
   724→| `lib/bar.ts` | ⚠️ No | - |
   725→
   726→**Coverage:** X/Y changed files have tests
   727→```
   728→
   729→### 2c. Change Tier Classification
   730→
   731→**Purpose:** Flag coverage gaps based on criticality, not just presence/absence.
   732→
   733→**Tier definitions:**
   734→
   735→| Tier | Change Type | Coverage Requirement |
   736→|------|-------------|---------------------|
   737→| **Critical** | Auth, payments, security, server-actions, middleware, admin | Flag prominently if missing |
   738→| **Standard** | Business logic, API handlers, utilities | Note if missing |
   739→| **Optional** | Config, types-only, UI tweaks | No flag needed |
   740→
   741→**Detection heuristic:**
   742→
   743→```bash
   744→# Detect critical paths in changed files
   745→changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
   746→critical=$(echo "$changed" | grep -E 'auth|payment|security|server-action|middleware|admin' || true)
   747→
   748→if [[ -n "$critical" ]]; then
   749→  echo "⚠️ CRITICAL PATH CHANGES (test coverage strongly recommended):"
   750→  echo "$critical"
   751→fi
   752→```
   753→
   754→**Reporting format:**
   755→
   756→```markdown
   757→### Change Tiers
   758→
   759→| Tier | Files | Coverage Status |
   760→|------|-------|-----------------|
   761→| Critical | `auth/login.ts` | ⚠️ NO TESTS - Flag prominently |
   762→| Standard | `lib/utils.ts` | Note: No tests |
   763→| Optional | `types/index.ts` | OK - Types only |
   764→```
   765→
   766→### 2d. Test Quality Review
   767→
   768→**When to apply:** Test files were added or modified.
   769→
   770→Evaluate test quality using the checklist:
   771→- **Behavior vs Implementation:** Tests assert on outputs, not internals
   772→- **Coverage Depth:** Error paths and edge cases covered
   773→- **Mock Hygiene:** Only external dependencies mocked
   774→- **Test Reliability:** No timing dependencies, deterministic
   775→
   776→See [test-quality-checklist.md](references/test-quality-checklist.md) for detailed evaluation criteria.
   777→
   778→**Flag common issues:**
   779→- Over-mocking (4+ modules mocked in single test)
   780→- Missing error path tests
   781→- Snapshot abuse (>50 line snapshots)
   782→- Implementation mirroring
   783→
   784→### 2e. Anti-Pattern Detection
   785→
   786→**Always run** code pattern checks on changed files:
   787→
   788→```bash
   789→# Get changed TypeScript/JavaScript files
   790→changed_files=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
   791→```
   792→
   793→**Check for:**
   794→
   795→| Category | Pattern | Risk |
   796→|----------|---------|------|
   797→| Performance | N+1 query (`await` in loop) | ⚠️ Medium |
   798→| Error Handling | Empty catch block | ⚠️ Medium |
   799→| Security | Hardcoded secrets | ❌ High |
   800→| Security | SQL concatenation | ❌ High |
   801→| Memory | Uncleared interval/timeout | ⚠️ Medium |
   802→| A11y | Image without alt | ⚠️ Low |
   803→
   804→**Dependency audit** (when `package.json` modified):
   805→
   806→| Flag | Threshold |
   807→|------|-----------|
   808→| Low downloads | <1,000/week |
   809→| Stale | No updates 12+ months |
   810→| License risk | UNLICENSED, GPL in MIT |
   811→| Security | Known vulnerabilities |
   812→
   813→See [anti-pattern-detection.md](references/anti-pattern-detection.md) for detection commands and full criteria.
   814→
   815→### 2f. Call-Site Review (When New Functions Added)
   816→
   817→**When to apply:** New exported functions are detected in the diff.
   818→
   819→**Purpose:** Review not just the function implementation but **where** and **how** it's called. A function can be perfectly implemented but called incorrectly at the call site. (Origin: Issue #295 — `rebaseBeforePR()` had thorough unit tests but was called for every issue in a chain loop when the AC specified "only the final branch.")
   820→
   821→**Detection:**
   822→```bash
   823→# Find new exported functions (added lines only)
   824→# Catches: export function foo, export async function foo,
   825→#          export const foo = () =>, export const foo = async () =>
   826→fn_exports=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E '^\+export (async )?function \w+' | sed 's/^+//' | grep -oE 'function \w+' | awk '{print $2}' || true)
   827→arrow_exports=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E '^\+export const \w+ = (async )?\(' | sed 's/^+//' | grep -oE 'const \w+' | awk '{print $2}' || true)
   828→new_exports=$(echo -e "${fn_exports}\n${arrow_exports}" | sed '/^$/d' | sort -u)
   829→export_count=$(echo "$new_exports" | grep -c . || echo 0)
   830→
   831→if [[ $export_count -gt 0 ]]; then
   832→  echo "New exported functions detected: $export_count"
   833→  echo "$new_exports"
   834→fi
   835→```
   836→
   837→**If new exported functions found:**
   838→
   839→#### Step 1: Call-Site Inventory
   840→
   841→For each new exported function, identify ALL call sites using the Grep tool:
   842→
   843→```
   844→# For each new function, find call sites
   845→# Use the Grep tool for each function name:
   846→Grep(pattern="${func}\\(", glob="*.{ts,tsx}", output_mode="content")
   847→# Then exclude test files, __tests__ dirs, and the export definition itself
   848→```
   849→
   850→**Call site types:**
   851→- Direct call: `functionName(args)`
   852→- Method call: `this.functionName(args)` or `obj.functionName(args)`
   853→- Callback: `.then(functionName)` or `array.map(functionName)`
   854→- Conditional: `condition && functionName(args)`
   855→
   856→#### Step 2: Condition Audit
   857→
   858→For each call site, document the conditions that gate the call:
   859→
   860→| Condition Type | Example | Check |
   861→|----------------|---------|-------|
   862→| Guard clause | `if (x) { fn() }` | Does condition match AC? |
   863→| Logical AND | `x && fn()` | Is guard sufficient? |
   864→| Ternary | `x ? fn() : null` | Correct branch? |
   865→| Early return | `if (!x) return; fn()` | Correct logic? |
   866→
   867→**Compare conditions against AC constraints:**
   868→- AC says "only when X" → Call site should have `if (X)` guard
   869→- AC says "not in Y mode" → Call site should have `if (!Y)` guard
   870→- AC says "for Z items" → Call site should filter for Z condition
   871→
   872→#### Step 3: Loop Awareness
   873→
   874→**Detect if function is called inside a loop:**
   875→
   876→```
   877→# For each function, use the Grep tool with context to check surrounding lines:
   878→Grep(pattern="${func}\\(", glob="*.{ts,tsx}", output_mode="content", -B=5)
   879→# Then inspect the context lines for loop constructs:
   880→# for, while, forEach, .map(, .filter(, .reduce(
   881→# If a loop is found, flag: "function called inside loop - verify iteration scope"
   882→```
   883→
   884→**Loop iteration review questions:**
   885→1. Should function run for ALL iterations? → OK if yes
   886→2. Should function run for FIRST/LAST only? → Check for index guard
   887→3. Should function run for SOME iterations? → Check for condition filter
   888→
   889→**Red flags:**
   890→- Function called unconditionally in loop when AC says "only once"
   891→- No break/return after call when AC implies single execution
   892→- Missing mode/flag guard when AC specifies conditions
   893→
   894→#### Step 4: Mode Sensitivity
   895→
   896→If the function accepts configuration or mode options:
   897→- Is the correct mode passed at the call site?
   898→- Are all mode-specific paths exercised appropriately?
   899→
   900→**Output Format:**
   901→
   902→```markdown
   903→### Call-Site Review
   904→
   905→**New exported functions detected:** N
   906→
   907→| Function | Call Sites | Loop? | Conditions | AC Match |
   908→|----------|-----------|-------|------------|----------|
   909→| `newFunction()` | `file.ts:123` | No | `if (success)` | ✅ Matches AC-2 |
   910→| `anotherFunc()` | `run.ts:456` | Yes | None | ⚠️ Missing guard (AC-3 says "final only") |
   911→| `thirdFunc()` | Not called | - | - | ⚠️ Unused export |
   912→
   913→**Findings:**
   914→- [List any mismatches between call-site conditions and AC constraints]
   915→
   916→**Recommendations:**
   917→- [Specific fixes needed at call sites]
   918→```
   919→
   920→**Verdict Impact:**
   921→
   922→| Finding | Verdict Impact |
   923→|---------|----------------|
   924→| All call sites match AC | No impact |
   925→| Call site missing AC-required guard | `AC_NOT_MET` |
   926→| Function not called anywhere | `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` (dead export) |
   927→| Call site in loop, AC unclear about iteration | `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   928→
   929→See [call-site-review.md](references/call-site-review.md) for detailed methodology and examples.
   930→
   931→### 3. QA vs AC
   932→
   933→For each AC item, mark as:
   934→- `MET`
   935→- `PARTIALLY_MET`
   936→- `NOT_MET`
   937→
   938→Provide a sentence or two explaining why.
   939→
   940→### 3a. AC Status Persistence — REQUIRED
   941→
   942→**After evaluating each AC item**, update the status in workflow state using the state CLI:
   943→
   944→```bash
   945→# Step 1: Initialize AC items for the issue (run once, before updating statuses)
   946→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts init-ac <issue-number> <ac-count>
   947→
   948→# Example: Initialize 4 AC items for issue #250
   949→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts init-ac 250 4
   950→```
   951→
   952→```bash
   953→# Step 2: Update each AC item's status
   954→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac <issue-number> <ac-id> <status> "<notes>"
   955→
   956→# Examples:
   957→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-1 met "Verified: tests pass and feature works"
   958→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-2 not_met "Missing error handling for edge case"
   959→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-3 blocked "Waiting on upstream dependency"
   960→```
   961→
   962→**Status mapping:**
   963→- `MET` → `met`
   964→- `PARTIALLY_MET` → `not_met` (with notes explaining what's missing)
   965→- `NOT_MET` → `not_met`
   966→- `BLOCKED` → `blocked` (external dependency issue)
   967→
   968→**Why this matters:** Updating AC status in state enables:
   969→- Dashboard shows real-time AC progress per issue
   970→- Cross-skill tracking of which AC items need work
   971→- Summary badges show "X/Y met" status
   972→
   973→**If issue has no stored AC:**
   974→- Run `init-ac` first to create the AC items
   975→- Then update each AC status individually
   976→
   977→### 4. Failure Path & Edge Case Testing (REQUIRED)
   978→
   979→Before any READY_FOR_MERGE verdict, complete the adversarial thinking checklist:
   980→
   981→1. **"What would break this?"** - Identify and test at least 2 failure scenarios
   982→2. **"What assumptions am I making?"** - List and validate key assumptions
   983→3. **"What's the unhappy path?"** - Test invalid inputs, failed dependencies
   984→4. **"Did I test the feature's PRIMARY PURPOSE?"** - If it handles errors, trigger an error
   985→
   986→See [testing-requirements.md](references/testing-requirements.md) for edge case checklists.
   987→
   988→### 5. Adversarial Self-Evaluation (REQUIRED)
   989→
   990→**Before issuing your verdict**, you MUST complete this adversarial self-evaluation to catch issues that automated quality checks miss.
   991→
   992→**Why this matters:** QA automation catches type issues, deleted tests, and scope creep - but misses:
   993→- Features that don't actually work as expected
   994→- Tests that pass but don't test the right things
   995→- Edge cases only apparent when actually using the feature
   996→
   997→**Answer these questions honestly:**
   998→1. "Did the implementation actually work when I reviewed it, or am I assuming it works?"
   999→2. "Do the tests actually test the feature's primary purpose, or just pass?"
  1000→3. "What's the most likely way this feature could break in production?"
  1001→4. "Am I giving a positive verdict because the code looks clean, or because I verified it works?"
  1002→
  1003→**Include this section in your output:**
  1004→
  1005→```markdown
  1006→### Self-Evaluation
  1007→
  1008→- **Verified working:** [Yes/No - did you actually verify the feature works, or assume it does?]
  1009→- **Test efficacy:** [High/Medium/Low - do tests catch the feature breaking?]
  1010→- **Likely failure mode:** [What would most likely break this in production?]
  1011→- **Verdict confidence:** [High/Medium/Low - explain any uncertainty]
  1012→```
  1013→
  1014→**If any answer reveals concerns:**
  1015→- Factor the concerns into your verdict
  1016→- If significant, change verdict to `AC_NOT_MET` or `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`
  1017→- Document the concerns in the QA comment
  1018→
  1019→**Do NOT skip this self-evaluation.** Honest reflection catches issues that code review cannot.
  1020→
  1021→#### Skill Change Review (Conditional)
  1022→
  1023→**When to apply:** `.claude/skills/**/*.md` files were modified.
  1024→
  1025→**Detect skill changes:**
  1026→```bash
  1027→skills_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^\.claude/skills/.*\.md$" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1028→```
  1029→
  1030→**If skills_changed > 0, add these adversarial prompts:**
  1031→
  1032→| Prompt | Why It Matters |
  1033→|--------|----------------|
  1034→| **Command verified:** Did you execute at least one referenced command? | Skill instructions can reference commands that don't work (wrong flags, missing fields) |
  1035→| **Fields verified:** For JSON commands, do field names match actual output? | Issue #178: `gh pr checks --json conclusion` failed because `conclusion` doesn't exist |
  1036→| **Patterns complete:** What variations might users write that aren't covered? | Skills define patterns - missing coverage causes silent failures |
  1037→| **Dependencies explicit:** What CLIs/tools does this skill assume are installed? | Missing `gh`, `npm`, etc. breaks the skill with confusing errors |
  1038→
  1039→**Example skill-specific self-evaluation:**
  1040→
  1041→```markdown
  1042→### Skill Change Review
  1043→
  1044→- [ ] **Command verified:** Executed `gh pr checks --json name,state,bucket` - fields exist ✅
  1045→- [ ] **Fields verified:** Checked `gh pr checks --help` for valid JSON fields ✅
  1046→- [ ] **Patterns complete:** Covered SUCCESS, FAILURE, PENDING states ✅
  1047→- [ ] **Dependencies explicit:** Requires `gh` CLI authenticated ✅
  1048→```
  1049→
  1050→---
  1051→
  1052→### 6. Execution Evidence (REQUIRED for scripts/CLI)
  1053→
  1054→**When to apply:** `scripts/` or CLI files were modified.
  1055→
  1056→**Detect change type:**
  1057→```bash
  1058→scripts_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^scripts/" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1059→cli_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "(cli|commands?)" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1060→```
  1061→
  1062→**If scripts/CLI changed, execute at least one smoke command:**
  1063→
  1064→| Change Type | Required Command |
  1065→|-------------|------------------|
  1066→| `scripts/` | `npx tsx scripts/<file>.ts --help` |
  1067→| CLI commands | `npx sequant <cmd> --help` or `--dry-run` |
  1068→| Tests only | `npm test -- --grep "feature"` |
  1069→| Types/config only | Waiver with reason |
  1070→
  1071→**Capture evidence:**
  1072→```bash
  1073→# Execute and capture
  1074→npx tsx scripts/example.ts --help 2>&1
  1075→echo "Exit code: $?"
  1076→```
  1077→
  1078→**Evidence status:**
  1079→- **Complete:** All required commands executed successfully
  1080→- **Incomplete:** Some commands not run or failed
  1081→- **Waived:** Explicit reason documented (types-only, config-only)
  1082→- **Not Required:** No executable changes
  1083→
  1084→**Verdict gating:**
  1085→- `READY_FOR_MERGE` requires evidence status: Complete, Waived, or Not Required
  1086→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` if evidence is Incomplete
  1087→
  1088→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed evidence requirements.
  1089→
  1090→---
  1091→
  1092→### 6a. Skill Command Verification (REQUIRED for skill changes)
  1093→
  1094→**When to apply:** `.claude/skills/**/*.md` files were modified.
  1095→
  1096→**Purpose:** Skills contain instructions with CLI commands. If those commands have wrong syntax, missing flags, or non-existent JSON fields, the skill will fail when used. QA must verify commands actually work before READY_FOR_MERGE.
  1097→
  1098→**Detect skill changes:**
  1099→```bash
  1100→skills_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^\.claude/skills/.*\.md$" || true)
  1101→skill_count=$(echo "$skills_changed" | grep -c . || echo 0)
  1102→```
  1103→
  1104→**Pre-requisite check:**
  1105→```bash
  1106→# Verify gh CLI is available before running verification
  1107→if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
  1108→  echo "⚠️ gh CLI not installed - skill command verification skipped"
  1109→  echo "Install: https://cli.github.com/"
  1110→  # Set verification status to "Skipped" with reason
  1111→fi
  1112→```
  1113→
  1114→**If skill_count > 0, extract and verify commands:**
  1115→
  1116→#### Step 1: Extract Commands from Changed Skills
  1117→
  1118→```bash
  1119→# Extract command patterns from skill files
  1120→for skill_file in $skills_changed; do
  1121→  echo "=== Commands in $skill_file ==="
  1122→
  1123→  # Commands at start of line (simple commands)
  1124→  grep -E '^\s*(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1125→
  1126→  # Commands in subshells/variable assignments: result=$(gh pr view ...)
  1127→  grep -oE '\$\((gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^)]+\)' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1128→
  1129→  # Commands in inline backticks
  1130→  grep -oE '\`(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^\`]+\`' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1131→
  1132→  # Commands after pipe or semicolon: ... | gh ... or ; npm ...
  1133→  grep -oE '[|;]\s*(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^|;&]+' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1134→done
  1135→```
  1136→
  1137→**Note:** Multi-line commands (using `\` continuation) require manual review. The extraction patterns above capture single-line commands only.
  1138→
  1139→#### Step 2: Verify Command Syntax
  1140→
  1141→For each extracted command type:
  1142→
  1143→| Command Type | Verification Method | Example |
  1144→|--------------|---------------------|---------|
  1145→| `gh pr checks --json X` | Check `gh pr checks --help` for valid JSON fields | `gh pr checks --help \| grep -A 30 "JSON FIELDS"` |
  1146→| `gh issue view --json X` | Check `gh issue view --help` for valid JSON fields | `gh issue view --help \| grep -A 30 "JSON FIELDS"` |
  1147→| `gh api ...` | Verify endpoint format matches GitHub API | Check endpoint structure |
  1148→| `npm run <script>` | Verify script exists in package.json | `jq '.scripts["<script>"]' package.json` |
  1149→| `npx tsx <file>` | Verify file exists | `test -f <file>` |
  1150→| `git <cmd>` | Verify against `git <cmd> --help` | Check valid flags |
  1151→
  1152→**JSON Field Validation Example:**
  1153→
  1154→```bash
  1155→# For commands like: gh pr checks --json name,state,conclusion
  1156→# Verify each field exists
  1157→
  1158→# Get valid fields
  1159→valid_fields=$(gh pr checks --help 2>/dev/null | grep -A 50 "JSON FIELDS" | grep -E "^\s+\w+" | awk '{print $1}' || true)
  1160→
  1161→# Check if "conclusion" is valid (spoiler: it's not)
  1162→echo "$valid_fields" | grep -qw "conclusion" && echo "✅ conclusion exists" || echo "❌ conclusion NOT a valid field"
  1163→```
  1164→
  1165→#### Step 3: Handle Placeholders
  1166→
  1167→Commands with placeholders (`<issue-number>`, `$PR_NUMBER`, `${VAR}`) cannot be executed directly.
  1168→
  1169→**Handling:**
  1170→- **Skip execution** for commands with placeholders
  1171→- **Mark as "Syntax verified, execution skipped"**
  1172→- **Still verify JSON fields** by extracting field names
  1173→
  1174→```bash
  1175→# Example: gh pr checks $pr_number --json name,state,bucket
  1176→# Can't execute (no $pr_number), but can verify fields
  1177→echo "name,state,bucket" | tr ',' '\n' | while read field; do
  1178→  gh pr checks --help | grep -qw "$field" && echo "✅ $field" || echo "❌ $field"
  1179→done
  1180→```
  1181→
  1182→#### Step 4: Command Verification Status
  1183→
  1184→| Status | Meaning |
  1185→|--------|---------|
  1186→| **Passed** | All commands verified, fields exist |
  1187→| **Failed** | At least one command has invalid syntax or non-existent fields |
  1188→| **Skipped** | Commands have placeholders; syntax looks valid but not executed |
  1189→| **Not Required** | No skill files changed |
  1190→
  1191→#### Verdict Gating
  1192→
  1193→**CRITICAL:** If skill command verification = **Failed**, verdict CANNOT be `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1194→
  1195→| Verification Status | Maximum Verdict |
  1196→|---------------------|-----------------|
  1197→| Passed | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1198→| Skipped | READY_FOR_MERGE (with note about unverified placeholders) |
  1199→| Failed | AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (blocks merge until fixed) |
  1200→| Not Required | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1201→
  1202→**Output Format:**
  1203→
  1204→```markdown
  1205→### Skill Command Verification
  1206→
  1207→**Skill files changed:** 2
  1208→
  1209→| File | Commands Found | Verification Status |
  1210→|------|----------------|---------------------|
  1211→| `.claude/skills/qa/SKILL.md` | 5 | ✅ Passed |
  1212→| `.claude/skills/exec/SKILL.md` | 3 | ⚠️ Skipped (placeholders) |
  1213→
  1214→**Commands Verified:**
  1215→- `gh pr checks --json name,state,bucket` → ✅ All fields exist
  1216→- `gh issue view --json title,body` → ✅ All fields exist
  1217→
  1218→**Commands with Issues:**
  1219→- `gh pr checks --json conclusion` → ❌ Field "conclusion" does not exist
  1220→
  1221→**Verification Status:** Failed
  1222→```
  1223→
  1224→---
  1225→
  1226→### 7. A+ Status Verdict
  1227→
  1228→Provide an overall verdict:
  1229→
  1230→- `READY_FOR_MERGE` — ALL ACs are `MET` and code quality is high ("A+")
  1231→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` — ALL ACs are `MET`, but meaningful improvements recommended
  1232→- `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` — ALL ACs are `MET` or `PENDING`, at least one requires external verification
  1233→- `AC_NOT_MET` — One or more ACs are `NOT_MET` or `PARTIALLY_MET`
  1234→
  1235→**Verdict Determination Algorithm (REQUIRED):**
  1236→
  1237→```text
  1238→1. Count AC statuses (INCLUDES both original AND derived ACs):
  1239→   - met_count = ACs with status MET (original + derived)
  1240→   - partial_count = ACs with status PARTIALLY_MET (original + derived)
  1241→   - pending_count = ACs with status PENDING (original + derived)
  1242→   - not_met_count = ACs with status NOT_MET (original + derived)
  1243→
  1244→   NOTE: Derived ACs are treated IDENTICALLY to original ACs.
  1245→   A derived AC marked NOT_MET will block merge just like an original AC.
  1246→
  1247→2. Check verification gates:
  1248→   - skill_verification = status from Section 6a (Passed/Failed/Skipped/Not Required)
  1249→   - execution_evidence = status from Section 6 (Complete/Incomplete/Waived/Not Required)
  1250→   - quality_plan_status = status from Phase 0b (Complete/Partial/Not Addressed/N/A)
  1251→
  1252→3. Browser testing enforcement check:
  1253→   - Check if any .tsx files were changed: git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep '\.tsx$' || true
  1254→   - Check if /test phase ran: look for test phase marker in issue comments
  1255→   - Check if issue has 'no-browser-test' label
  1256→   - IF .tsx files changed AND /test did NOT run AND no 'no-browser-test' label:
  1257→       → Set browser_test_missing = true
  1258→
  1259→4. Determine verdict (in order):
  1260→   - IF not_met_count > 0 OR partial_count > 0:
  1261→       → AC_NOT_MET (block merge)
  1262→   - ELSE IF skill_verification == "Failed":
  1263→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (skill commands have issues - cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1264→   - ELSE IF execution_evidence == "Incomplete":
  1265→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (scripts not verified - cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1266→   - ELSE IF quality_plan_status == "Not Addressed" AND quality_plan_exists:
  1267→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (quality dimensions not addressed - flag for review)
  1268→   - ELSE IF browser_test_missing (from step 3):
  1269→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (browser testing recommended for .tsx changes)
  1270→         Note: "Browser testing recommended: .tsx files modified without /test phase.
  1271→               Add 'ui' label to enable, or 'no-browser-test' to opt out."
  1272→   - ELSE IF pending_count > 0:
  1273→       → NEEDS_VERIFICATION (wait for verification)
  1274→   - ELSE IF quality_plan_status == "Partial":
  1275→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (some quality dimensions incomplete - can merge with notes)
  1276→   - ELSE IF improvement_suggestions.length > 0:
  1277→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (can merge with notes)
  1278→   - ELSE:
  1279→       → READY_FOR_MERGE (A+ implementation)
  1280→```
  1281→
  1282→**Browser Testing Enforcement:**
  1283→
  1284→Before finalizing the verdict, check for missing browser test coverage:
  1285→
  1286→```bash
  1287→# Check if .tsx files were changed
  1288→tsx_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep '\.tsx$' || true)
  1289→
  1290→# Check if /test phase ran (look for test phase marker in issue comments)
  1291→test_ran=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --json comments --jq '[.comments[].body]' | \
  1292→  grep -o '{"phase":"test"' || true)
  1293→
  1294→# Check for no-browser-test label
  1295→no_browser_test=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --json labels --jq '.labels[].name' | \
  1296→  grep 'no-browser-test' || true)
  1297→
  1298→if [[ -n "$tsx_changed" && -z "$test_ran" && -z "$no_browser_test" ]]; then
  1299→  echo "⚠️ Browser testing recommended: .tsx files modified without /test phase"
  1300→  # Force verdict to AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1301→fi
  1302→```
  1303→
  1304→| Condition | Verdict Effect |
  1305→|-----------|---------------|
  1306→| `.tsx` changed + `/test` ran | Normal verdict |
  1307→| `.tsx` changed + `no-browser-test` label | Normal verdict (explicit opt-out) |
  1308→| `.tsx` changed + no `/test` + no opt-out | Force `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` |
  1309→| No `.tsx` changed | Normal verdict |
  1310→
  1311→**CRITICAL:** `PARTIALLY_MET` is NOT sufficient for merge. It MUST be treated as `NOT_MET` for verdict purposes.
  1312→
  1313→**CRITICAL:** If skill command verification = "Failed", verdict CANNOT be `READY_FOR_MERGE`. This prevents shipping skills with broken commands (like issue #178's `conclusion` field).
  1314→
  1315→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed verdict criteria.
  1316→
  1317→---
  1318→
  1319→## Automated Quality Checks (Reference)
  1320→
  1321→**Note:** These commands are what the sub-agents execute internally. You do NOT run these directly — the sub-agents spawned above handle this. This section is reference documentation only.
  1322→
  1323→```bash
  1324→# Type safety
  1325→type_issues=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E ":\s*any[,)]|as any" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1326→
  1327→# Deleted tests
  1328→deleted_tests=$(git diff main...HEAD --diff-filter=D --name-only | grep -E "\\.test\\.|\\spec\\." | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1329→
  1330→# Scope check
  1331→files_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | wc -l | xargs)
  1332→
  1333→# Size check
  1334→additions=$(git diff main...HEAD --numstat | awk '{sum+=$1} END {print sum+0}')
  1335→deletions=$(git diff main...HEAD --numstat | awk '{sum+=$2} END {print sum+0}')
  1336→
  1337→# Security scan
  1338→npx tsx scripts/lib/__tests__/run-security-scan.ts 2>/dev/null
  1339→```
  1340→
  1341→See [scripts/quality-checks.sh](scripts/quality-checks.sh) for the complete automation script.
  1342→
  1343→### 8. Draft Review/QA Comment
  1344→
  1345→Produce a Markdown snippet for the PR/issue:
  1346→- Short summary of the change
  1347→- AC coverage status (bullet list)
  1348→- Key strengths and issues
  1349→- Clear, actionable next steps
  1350→
  1351→### 9. Update GitHub Issue
  1352→
  1353→**If orchestrated (SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR is set):**
  1354→- Skip posting GitHub comment (orchestrator handles aggregated summary)
  1355→- Include verdict and AC coverage in output for orchestrator to capture
  1356→- Let orchestrator update labels based on final workflow status
  1357→
  1358→**If standalone:**
  1359→
  1360→Post the draft comment to GitHub and update labels:
  1361→
  1362→- `AC_NOT_MET`: add `needs-work` label
  1363→- `READY_FOR_MERGE`: add `ready-for-review` label
  1364→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`: add `needs-improvement` label
  1365→- `NEEDS_VERIFICATION`: add `needs-verification` label
  1366→
  1367→### 10. Documentation Reminder
  1368→
  1369→If verdict is `READY_FOR_MERGE` or `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`:
  1370→
  1371→```md
  1372→**Documentation:** Before merging, run `/docs <issue>` to generate feature documentation.
  1373→```
  1374→
  1375→### 10a. CHANGELOG Quality Gate (REQUIRED)
  1376→
  1377→**Purpose:** Verify user-facing changes have corresponding CHANGELOG entries before `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1378→
  1379→**Detection:**
  1380→
  1381→```bash
  1382→# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
  1383→if [ ! -f "CHANGELOG.md" ]; then
  1384→  echo "No CHANGELOG.md found - skip CHANGELOG check"
  1385→  exit 0
  1386→fi
  1387→
  1388→# Check if [Unreleased] section has entries
  1389→unreleased_entries=$(sed -n '/^## \[Unreleased\]/,/^## \[/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -E '^\s*-' | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1390→
  1391→# Determine if change is user-facing (new features, bug fixes, etc.)
  1392→# Look at commit messages or file changes
  1393→user_facing=$(git log main..HEAD --oneline | grep -iE '^[a-f0-9]+ (feat|fix|perf|refactor|docs):' | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1394→```
  1395→
  1396→**Verification Logic:**
  1397→
  1398→| Condition | CHANGELOG Entry Required? | Action |
  1399→|-----------|---------------------------|--------|
  1400→| User-facing changes detected + CHANGELOG exists | ✅ Yes | Check for `[Unreleased]` entry |
  1401→| User-facing changes + no entry | ⚠️ Block | Flag as missing CHANGELOG |
  1402→| Non-user-facing changes (test, ci, chore) | ❌ No | Skip check |
  1403→| No CHANGELOG.md in repo | ❌ No | Skip check |
  1404→
  1405→**If CHANGELOG entry is missing:**
  1406→
  1407→1. Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` verdict
  1408→2. Set verdict to `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` with note:
  1409→   ```markdown
  1410→   **CHANGELOG:** Missing entry for user-facing changes. Add entry to `## [Unreleased]` section before merging.
  1411→   ```
  1412→3. Include this in the draft review comment
  1413→
  1414→**CHANGELOG Entry Validation:**
  1415→
  1416→When an entry exists, verify it follows the format:
  1417→- Starts with action verb (Add, Fix, Update, Remove, Improve)
  1418→- Includes issue number `(#123)`
  1419→- Is under the correct section (Added, Fixed, Changed, etc.)
  1420→
  1421→**Example validation:**
  1422→
  1423→```markdown
  1424→### CHANGELOG Verification
  1425→
  1426→| Check | Status |
  1427→|-------|--------|
  1428→| CHANGELOG.md exists | ✅ Found |
  1429→| User-facing changes | ✅ Yes (feat: commit detected) |
  1430→| [Unreleased] entry | ✅ Present |
  1431→| Entry format | ✅ Valid (includes issue number) |
  1432→
  1433→**Result:** CHANGELOG requirements met
  1434→```
  1435→
  1436→**If CHANGELOG is not required:**
  1437→
  1438→```markdown
  1439→### CHANGELOG Verification
  1440→
  1441→**Result:** N/A (non-user-facing changes only)
  1442→```
  1443→
  1444→---
  1445→
  1446→### 11. Script/CLI Execution Verification
  1447→
  1448→**REQUIRED for CLI/script features:** When `scripts/` files are modified, execution verification is required before `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1449→
  1450→**Detection:**
  1451→```bash
  1452→scripts_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^(scripts/|templates/scripts/)" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1453→if [[ $scripts_changed -gt 0 ]]; then
  1454→  echo "Script changes detected. Run /verify before READY_FOR_MERGE"
  1455→fi
  1456→```
  1457→
  1458→**Verification evidence:** Look for "Execution Verification" section in issue comments. This section is posted by the `/verify` skill and includes:
  1459→- Command executed
  1460→- Exit code and duration
  1461→- Output sample (truncated)
  1462→- Human confirmation of expected behavior
  1463→
  1464→**If no verification evidence exists:**
  1465→1. Prompt: "Script changes detected but no execution verification found. Run `/verify <issue> --command \"<test command>\"` before READY_FOR_MERGE verdict."
  1466→2. Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` verdict until verification is complete (unless an approved override applies — see Section 11a)
  1467→3. Verdict should be `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` with note about missing verification
  1468→
  1469→**Why this matters:**
  1470→- Code review + unit tests miss integration issues
  1471→- CLI features must be executed at least once to verify end-to-end behavior
  1472→
  1473→**Example workflow:**
  1474→```bash
  1475→# QA detects scripts/ or templates/scripts/ changes
  1476→# -> Prompt: "Run /verify before READY_FOR_MERGE"
  1477→
  1478→/verify 558 --command "npx tsx scripts/migrate.ts --dry-run"
  1479→
  1480→# Human confirms output looks correct
  1481→# -> /verify posts evidence to issue
  1482→
  1483→/qa 558  # Re-run, now sees verification, can give READY_FOR_MERGE
  1484→```
  1485→
  1486→### 11a. Script Verification Override
  1487→
  1488→In some cases, `/verify` execution can be safely skipped when script changes are purely cosmetic or have no runtime impact. **Overrides require explicit justification and risk assessment.**
  1489→
  1490→**Override Format (REQUIRED when skipping /verify):**
  1491→
  1492→```markdown
  1493→### Script Verification Override
  1494→
  1495→**Requirement:** `/verify` before READY_FOR_MERGE
  1496→**Override:** Yes
  1497→**Justification:** [One of the approved categories below]
  1498→**Risk Assessment:** [None/Low/Medium]
  1499→```
  1500→
  1501→**Approved Override Categories:**
  1502→
  1503→| Category | Example | Risk |
  1504→|----------|---------|------|
  1505→| Syntax-only refactor | `catch (error)` → `catch` | None |
  1506→| Comment/documentation changes | Adding JSDoc, updating comments | None |
  1507→| Type annotation additions | Adding `: string`, `: number` | None |
  1508→| Import reorganization | Sorting imports, removing unused | None |
  1509→| Variable rename (no logic change) | `foo` → `bar` with no behavioral change | Low |
  1510→| Dead code removal | Removing unreachable branches | Low |
  1511→
  1512→**NOT Approved for Override (always require /verify):**
  1513→
  1514→| Category | Example | Why |
  1515→|----------|---------|-----|
  1516→| Logic changes | Modified conditionals, new branches | Runtime behavior changes |
  1517→| New functionality | Added functions, new exports | Must verify execution |
  1518→| Dependency changes | Updated imports from new packages | May affect runtime |
  1519→| Error handling changes | Modified catch blocks, new try/catch | Failure paths change |
  1520→| Configuration changes | Modified env vars, config parsing | Environment-dependent |
  1521→
  1522→**Risk Assessment Definitions:**
  1523→
  1524→| Level | Meaning | Criteria |
  1525→|-------|---------|----------|
  1526→| **None** | Zero runtime impact | Change is invisible at runtime (comments, types, syntax) |
  1527→| **Low** | Negligible runtime impact | Change is cosmetic (rename, dead code) with no logical effect |
  1528→| **Medium** | Possible runtime impact | Change touches executable code but appears safe — **should NOT be overridden** |
  1529→
  1530→**Override Decision Flow:**
  1531→
  1532→1. Check if change matches an approved category → If no, `/verify` is required
  1533→2. Assess risk level → If Medium or higher, `/verify` is required
  1534→3. Document override using the format above in the QA output
  1535→4. Include override in the GitHub issue comment for audit trail
  1536→
  1537→**CRITICAL:** When in doubt, run `/verify`. Overrides are for clear-cut cases only. If you need to argue that a change is safe, it probably needs verification.
  1538→
  1539→---

  1543→**IMPORTANT:** Update workflow state when running standalone (not orchestrated).

  1565→**Why this matters:** State tracking enables dashboard visibility, resume capability, and workflow orchestration. Skills update state when standalone; orchestrators handle state when running workflows.

  1596→### Early Exit (No Implementation)

  1603→- [ ] **Sub-agents NOT spawned** - Quality check agents were skipped

  1605→**DO NOT respond until all applicable items are verified.**

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
